title: Vantara and Hanseong Announce Licensing Partnership, Not Acquisition
body: Vantara AI and South Korea's Hanseong Group announced Friday that they have signed a technology licensing agreement, pushing back against reports from Korean media earlier this week that described a full acquisition. Under the deal, Hanseong will license Vantara's retail analytics platform for use across its Asia-Pacific operations for an initial period of five years. Financial terms were not disclosed. Vantara CEO Nils Bergström said the partnership "opens a significant new market" for the company without requiring it to give up operational independence. A spokesperson for Hanseong confirmed that no acquisition discussions are ongoing, contradicting an earlier report in Korea Finance Today. Ji-Yeon Kwon, Hanseong's CFO, was cited in an internal memo obtained by TechBrief Europe as the architect of the licensing structure. The company's shares rose slightly on the news. The announcement made no mention of the continuing GDPR inquiry in Ireland, which some investors said remained an overhang.
